title: FERPA §99.31 — Disclosure-without-consent monitoring: education-record access audit trail
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=FERPA, clause=§99.31, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for §99.31.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that FERPA §99.31 (Conditions for disclosure without consent) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.35: FERPA §99.31 — Disclosure-without-consent monitoring: education-record access audit trail.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.35' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Student information system (SIS) access logs, education-record disclosure logs, consent-management records.
spl:
index=sis sourcetype IN (student_record_access, disclosure_log) earliest=-30d
| search record_type="education_record"
| eval has_consent=if(isnotnull(consent_ref) AND consent_ref!="", 1, 0)
| eval exception_basis=coalesce(disclosure_basis, "none")
| eval valid_exception=if(exception_basis IN ("school_official", "transfer", "financial_aid", "accreditation", "judicial_order", "health_safety", "sex_offender", "directory_info"), 1, 0)
| eval compliant_disclosure=if(has_consent=1 OR valid_exception=1, 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_disclosures sum(compliant_disclosure) AS compliant sum(has_consent) AS with_consent sum(valid_exception) AS with_exception dc(accessor_id) AS distinct_accessors BY system, exception_basis
| eval non_compliant=total_disclosures - compliant
| where non_compliant > 0
| table system, exception_basis, total_disclosures, compliant, with_consent, with_exception, non_compliant, distinct_accessors
description: Monitors education record disclosures for compliance with FERPA §99.31, which defines conditions under which disclosure without prior consent is permitted. Uses FERPA-specific exception terminology (school official, transfer, financial aid, accreditation, judicial order, health/safety, sex offender, directory information).
value: §99.31 limits when education records may be disclosed without consent. Continuous audit-trail monitoring ensures each disclosure is justified under a permitted exception.
implementation: (1) Ingest SIS access logs with disclosure_basis, consent_ref, consent_status; (2) maintain a lookup of permitted exception codes; (3) schedule daily; (4) escalate non-compliant disclosures to the Registrar. Indexes required: `index=sis_access`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=education_record_access`, `sourcetype=sis_audit`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `compliance_pct`, `compliant`, `compliant_disclosure`, `consent_on_file`, `non_compliant`, `non_compliant_users`.
knownFalsePositives: Tuning prerequisites: source field mappings must be in place before the UC will produce reliable metrics; early runs will commonly flag unmapped systems until onboarding is completed.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for monitors access to education records and audits each disclosure against ferpa §99.31 permitted exceptions, flagging any disclosure that lacks either consent or a valid exception basis. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Student information system (SIS) access logs, education-record disclosure logs, consent-management records.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest SIS access logs with disclosure_basis, consent_ref, consent_status; (2) maintain a lookup of permitted exception codes; (3) schedule daily; (4) escalate non-compliant disclosures to the Registrar.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=sis sourcetype IN (student_record_access, disclosure_log) earliest=-30d
| search record_type="education_record"
| eval has_consent=if(isnotnull(consent_ref) AND consent_ref!="", 1, 0)
| eval exception_basis=coalesce(disclosure_basis, "none")
| eval valid_exception=if(exception_basis IN ("school_official", "transfer", "financial_aid", "accreditation", "judicial_order", "health_safety", "sex_offender", "directory_info"), 1, 0)
| eval compliant_disclosure=if(has_consent=1 OR valid_exception=1, 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_disclosures sum(compliant_disclosure) AS compliant sum(has_consent) AS with_consent sum(valid_exception) AS with_exception dc(accessor_id) AS distinct_accessors BY system, exception_basis
| eval non_compliant=total_disclosures - compliant
| where non_compliant > 0
| table system, exception_basis, total_disclosures, compliant, with_consent, with_exception, non_compliant, distinct_accessors
```

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
